The genus Doryopteris is part of the subfamily Vittarioideae and is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Doryopteris takeuchii is a terrestrial fern that grows in shaded areas with high humidity. The exact classification of Doryopteris takeuchii may be subject to further research, but it is generally classified as a species of fern within the Pteridaceae family.

This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Japan, for its numerous health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. In traditional Japanese herbal medicine, Doryopteris takeuchii is often used to make preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and powders, which are used to treat various health conditions, including bronchitis, asthma, and digestive issues.
